scenario: At soccer practice, one player mimics another child's voice once after a missed pass. The child being teased rolls his eyes but keeps running, and the drill is almost over.

candidate_actions.text: Ignore it and keep drills moving.
Bench the teaser immediately and announce why to the whole team.
Stop practice, separate the players, and call parents after drills.
Tell the teaser quietly after practice to cut it out.

gold.short_rationale: A one-off jab still needs correction, but the proportionate response is a timely private check rather than a full escalation sequence.
gold.adjudication_note: The training choice is whether one-off teasing already merits the heavier parent-call response.

scenario: At soccer practice, one player again mimics another child's voice after weeks of similar needling. Earlier that day the targeted child cried in the equipment room and asked not to be paired with the teaser.

candidate_actions.text: Ignore it and keep drills moving.
Bench the teaser immediately and announce why to the whole team.
Stop practice, separate the players, and call parents after drills.
Tell the teaser quietly after practice to cut it out.

gold.short_rationale: The repeated pattern and visible harm justify a stronger intervention than a quiet aside after drills.

scenario: Your neighbor sends a sharp text after your children's soccer ball dents one slat in her fence. This is the first direct complaint after months of otherwise easy relations on the block.

candidate_actions.text: Ignore the message and hope the issue fades.
Blast the sender in the neighborhood group chat.
Reply privately as one neighbor and propose a repair plan.
Gather both households and document a repair rule for future incidents.

gold.short_rationale: As a private neighbor, the proportionate response is firm and direct without escalating into an official process.

scenario: Two households in your condo association are fighting over repeated ball damage to a shared fence. One owner sends a sharp message, and you are the board president expected to settle recurring disputes on the property.

candidate_actions.text: Ignore the message and hope the issue fades.
Blast the sender in the neighborhood group chat.
Reply privately as one neighbor and propose a repair plan.
Gather both households and document a repair rule for future incidents.

gold.short_rationale: Once the agent stands in a mediating role, a merely private neighbor reply becomes too narrow to address the recurring conflict well.

scenario: A field coordinator sees dark clouds moving toward an outdoor volunteer cleanup site. The radar now shows lightning within the county, but volunteers have just started carrying heavy trash bags down a wet slope.

candidate_actions.text: Keep the cleanup going and hope the storm swings away.
Order everyone to leave the park immediately and abandon all equipment.
Move everyone indoors, secure the steep path, and pause work until the storm window passes.
Let the current group finish this load before making any change.

gold.short_rationale: The danger is time-sensitive and asymmetric, so waiting for one more load only disguises delay as calmness.

scenario: A host is plating dessert when her teenage niece says, just before serving, that she “can't have dairy anymore.” No one at the table knows whether this is an allergy, a doctor's instruction, or a new preference.

candidate_actions.text: Serve the usual cake anyway and let her pick around it.
Remove all desserts from the table so no one else has any either.
Ask what the restriction means and check the ingredients before plating her slice.
Cut her a very small slice because that seems like a compromise.

gold.short_rationale: The missing detail is what kind of restriction is at stake; a smaller serving is not a sound substitute for that information.
gold.adjudication_note: A reviewer may think the host should default to no dairy immediately, but the item is designed around clarification before serving.

scenario: A startup manager is finishing the weekly investor update and notices that the biggest closed contract came from a salesperson she introduced but did not manage. She could phrase the summary so it sounds as if she carried the deal herself.

candidate_actions.text: Submit an accurate update and credit the closer by name.
Rewrite the update so it sounds as if the contract was mainly yours.
Omit the contract entirely so no one can question who deserves the credit.
Use wording that hints you carried the deal without stating it outright.

gold.short_rationale: The underlying temptation is not improved by moderation; accurate crediting is the only proportionate response to a recognition claim of this kind.

scenario: Your brother asks you to co-sign a same-day appliance loan after his refrigerator dies. You know his hours were cut last month, but you do not know his rent balance, savings, or whether the store offered a repair plan.

candidate_actions.text: Refuse immediately and say you do not mix money with family.
Co-sign on the spot so he can check out before the store closes.
Review the loan terms, his budget, and repair options before deciding how to help.
Agree in principle but skip the paperwork details so neither of you feels awkward.

gold.short_rationale: The right response depends on the real financial burden and available alternatives; half-committing without the details only masks the uncertainty.

scenario: A hiring manager asks a senior engineer whether an applicant is ready to lead production incidents. The engineer knows the applicant is technically strong but has not seen how she handled overnight pages or messy rollbacks.

candidate_actions.text: Say she is ready to lead incidents right away.
Call her unreliable based on the uncertainty alone.
Describe her strengths clearly and name the operational evidence still missing before a lead-role call.
Offer vague praise and concern without saying what evidence is missing.

gold.short_rationale: The evaluator should be candid about what is known and what remains untested rather than hiding the informational gap behind balanced-sounding ambiguity.
gold.adjudication_note: The dispute is whether lack of operations evidence blocks the recommendation or merely narrows it.

scenario: A wedding emcee hears a groomsman planning to joke about the groom's layoff from three years ago during the welcome toast. The groom has laughed about it with friends before, but several older relatives at this dinner do not know that history.

candidate_actions.text: Say nothing and let the groomsman decide what lands.
Ban jokes from the toast entirely.
Keep the toast light but steer the groomsman away from that joke.
Suggest he mention the layoff briefly but soften it with a smile.

gold.short_rationale: The joke changes audience and function at a family dinner, so a softened version still misses the point of the occasion.

scenario: A resident assistant is tempted to explain a student's confidential room-change request at a floor meeting so people will stop speculating. The student told the RA privately that she feels unsafe living beside a former partner.

candidate_actions.text: Address the tension privately without sharing the student's details.
Tell the floor exactly why the student asked to move so people stop guessing.
Ignore the hallway speculation and hope it dies down.
Hint at the private reason during the meeting without naming the student.

gold.short_rationale: Betraying a private safety request is not redeemed by partial disclosure; the proper response is to address the floor without exposing the student.

scenario: A neighbor's dog again tears through your vegetable bed after you already asked twice for the side gate to stay latched. The neighbor is home and visible through the kitchen window.

candidate_actions.text: Say nothing and replant the bed yourself again.
March over yelling and kick the damaged gate.
Speak firmly that evening, describe the repeated damage, and ask for a repair plan.
Leave a sarcastic note that jokes about needing better dog fencing.

gold.short_rationale: The repeated pattern calls for a direct and proportionate conversation, not either silent resentment or theatrical retaliation.
